rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target implementation scope when binding to implementation-level outputs or subcomponent connections.
Place component-type-level contracts in the component type scope only when using visible features or explicitly declared variables.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture integrity by not introducing external or unresolved references into AGREE annexes.
Ensure AGREE expressions reference only visible ports, constants, or declared variables—not AADL property names.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **};
Use eq declarations to define bool expressions for range checks before referencing them in guarantees.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not use subcomponent paths or implementation-private names in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
